coverage_notes: Multi-turn stress test: each turn is a vague user message (no asset paths, no tool names, no procedural recipes). Intended to exercise discovery, blueprint authoring, graph editing, and cleanup without telling the agent how to do it.

turns.request: I'm new to this project. Pick any interesting asset you can find in the content and tell me what it is and whether you can open it.
I need a fresh actor blueprint for quick experiments—something I can drop in a level. Create one with a sensible name and parent it off a plain actor.
When that actor runs, I want to see a simple message in the log or on screen so I know it actually fired. Hook that up on startup.
Add an integer field on the actor to count how many times something happens, starting at zero.
Make another actor blueprint in the same vein—related purpose, different asset—so I can compare them side by side.
On one of those actors, add a custom event I could call later from elsewhere, and have it do something visible when it runs.
The event graph is getting hard to follow. Reroute the main execution flow so it's clearer without breaking what runs at start.
Add two integers: a cap for how many jumps or tries are allowed, and a counter for how many have been used. Branch so we only continue when the counter is still under the cap.
Tidy up the graph layout so it isn't a mess—whatever approach fits.
Give me a short recap of which assets we touched in this thread and whether anything is still complaining in the compiler.
